issues.detail: Example is corrupted: text is truncated German poem fragment ('Du häßlicher Vogel, wirst du einst') and the translation field contains more German text ('Und hacke dir ab die Krallen.') instead of an English translation. No annotations. Unusable example.
Translation 'I was born in bacon.' is wrong — Speck here is a place name (proper noun), not the common noun 'bacon'. Should be 'I was born in Speck.'

Annotation for 'Speck' has lemma 'Speck' pos 'noun' gloss_hint 'body fat' — wrong. Speck here is a proper noun (place name). Should be pos: 'proper noun' or 'name', and gloss_hint should reference the place sense.
Annotation for 'Ich' has lemma 'ich' pos 'noun' gloss_hint 'we' — wrong POS and wrong gloss_hint. 'Ich' is a pronoun meaning 'I', not a noun meaning 'we'.
Translation 'I spent my vacation in bacon.' is wrong — Speck here is a place name. Should be 'I spent my vacation in Speck.'
Annotation for 'Speck' has lemma 'Speck' pos 'noun' gloss_hint 'body fat' — wrong, should be proper noun (place name). Annotation for 'Ich' has pos 'noun' gloss_hint 'we' — should be pronoun 'I'.

Translation 'Many paths lead to Rome.' completely changes the meaning. The German 'Nach Speck führen viele Wege.' means 'Many paths lead to Speck.' (the place). This is not the proverb about Rome — it's a template sentence for the place name Speck.

All annotations use English forms from the translation text instead of German forms from the original sentence. E.g. form 'torn open' instead of 'aufgerissen', form 'drawers' instead of 'Schubladen', form 'cabinets' instead of 'Schränke'. All annotations need to reference the German source text.
Annotation for 'Kleine' has gloss_hint 'girl' — wrong, 'Der Kleine' (masculine article) means 'the little boy', not girl.
Annotation for 'aufgerissen' has gloss_hint 'tear open' — in this architectural context (drawing a facade elevation), the sense is 'sketch elevation' not 'tear open'.
Annotation for 'Betenden' has lemma 'beten' pos 'verb' — 'Betenden' is a substantivized participle functioning as a noun here (die Betenden = the worshippers/praying people), not a verb form.
Annotation for 'schaden' has lemma 'Schaden' pos 'noun' — wrong, 'schaden' here is the verb 'to harm' (infinitive), not the noun 'Schaden' (damage). Should be lemma 'schaden', pos 'verb'.

Annotation for 'einzugeben' has gloss_hint 'think' — inaccurate for this sense. The sense is 'persuade/suggest' (sense 2: bei jemandem bewirken, dass dieser etwas glaubt), not 'think'. A better gloss_hint would be 'persuade'.

Duplicate annotations: 'eingeben', 'würden', 'Sie', 'mich', 'dafür' each appear twice in the annotation list. Also 'eingeben' gloss_hint 'assign' doesn't match the sense — this is the 'nominate' sense.
Translation 'that is just a foundation' is wrong for this sense. The sense is 'trifle/something insignificant'. Should be something like 'that is just a trifle' or 'that's nothing'.
Annotation for 'Stift' has gloss_hint 'foundation' — wrong sense. This is the Stift_etwas file (sense 0: trifle/insignificant thing). gloss_hint should match 'trifle'.

All annotations use English lemmas instead of German: 'boy' instead of 'Bursche', 'small' instead of 'klein', 'grow' instead of 'wachsen', 'that' instead of 'dass', 'he' instead of 'er', 'everyone' instead of 'alle', 'simply' instead of 'einfach', 'pen' instead of 'Stift'. Entire annotation set needs German lemmas.
Translation says 'everyone simply calls him Pen' — but in this context Stift means 'boy of small stature' (sense 2), not 'Pen'. Should be something like 'everyone simply calls him Shorty/kid'.

All annotations use English forms from the translation ('the', 'home', 'pens', 'were', 'constantly', 'chewed', 'by', 'big') instead of German forms ('Im', 'Heim', 'Stifte', 'wurden', 'pausenlos', 'gemorscht', 'von', 'großen'). Entire annotation set references the English translation text.

Translation 'the pens were constantly chewed by the big Oukies' is wrong — Stifte here means children/kids (sense 3: Kind), not pens. Should be 'the little kids were constantly bullied/beaten by the big Oukies'.

Annotation for 'junge' has lemma 'junge' pos 'noun' gloss_hint 'boy' — wrong. Here 'junge' is the adjective 'jung' modifying 'Mann' (der junge Mann = the young man). Should be lemma 'jung', pos 'adjective'. Also 'Stift' gloss_hint 'pen' is wrong — this is the 'young man' sense.

Translation 'My pen has strangely asked me if I am his biological father.' is wrong — Stift here means 'son' (sense 5), not pen. Should be 'My son has strangely asked me if I am his biological father.'
Annotation for 'Stift' has gloss_hint 'pen' — wrong sense. Should reference the 'son' sense.

Annotation for 'Stift' has gloss_hint 'pen' — wrong sense. In context ('Stift im Hotel Waldrast'), this means apprentice (sense 6). Also annotation has form 'kannte' but text says 'wußte' — wrong form. Annotation for 'geworden' but text has 'gewesen' — wrong form.
Translation says 'had once been a clerk' — the word Stift here means 'apprentice' not 'clerk'. Otherwise acceptable.
Annotation for 'Stifte' has gloss_hint 'pen' — wrong sense. In context (rescue operations, stable and robust), this means 'recruits' (sense 7). Translation 'these pens' should be 'these recruits'.
Translation 'these pens are stable and robust enough to complete the daily rescue operation' is wrong — Stifte here means recruits, not pens.
Annotation for 'Stifte' has gloss_hint 'pencil' — wrong sense. In context (die Schulbank drücken = start school), Stifte means 'schoolchildren/beginners' (sense 8).

Translation 'The pencils had to wait four days' is wrong — Stifte here means schoolchildren/new students, not pencils. Should be 'The new students had to wait four more days before sitting at the school bench for the first time.'

All annotations use English forms from the translation text instead of German forms. E.g. 'one', 'pens', 'parents', 'spend', 'euros' instead of 'einer', 'Stifte', 'Eltern', 'ausgeben', 'Euro'. Entire annotation set references English text.

Translation 'He is just one of the pens for which...' is wrong — Stifte here means tutees/students receiving tutoring (sense 9), not pens. Should be 'He is just one of the tutees for whom concerned parents spend more than one billion euros annually.'

Example is corrupted: text is truncated ('Hier geb ich ihm zwey Stuͤbchen ein,') and translation field contains more German text ('Und was er braucht, das ſoll zu ſeinen Dienſten ſeyn.'). No annotations. Unusable example.

All annotations use English forms from the translation text instead of German forms. E.g. 'The', 'food', 'must', 'be', 'dry', 'surface' instead of 'Das', 'Gargut', 'muss', 'sein', 'trocken', 'Oberfläche'. Entire annotation set references English text.

All annotations use English forms from the translation text instead of German forms. E.g. 'armed', 'man', 'comes', 'toilet', 'shot', 'blood', 'splatters'. Entire annotation set references English text.

Annotation for 'Schlamm' has English lemma 'mud' instead of German 'Schlamm'. Annotation for 'angezogen' has pos 'adjective' — should be verb (past participle of anziehen used as Partizip II in the infinitive construction).

All annotations use English forms from the translation text: 'Whenever', 'I', 'sprayed', 'pesticides', 'got', 'very', 'bad', 'headaches'. Should reference German text: 'Immer', 'ich', 'spritzte', 'Pflanzenschutzmittel', 'bekam', 'sehr', 'starke', 'Kopfschmerzen'.

Multiple annotations use English lemmas instead of German: 'farm' instead of 'Hof', 'his' instead of 'sein', 'father' instead of 'Vater', 'the' instead of 'der', 'community' instead of 'Gemeinde', 'and' instead of 'und', 'from' instead of 'von', 'until' instead of 'bis', 'fruit trees' instead of 'Obstbaum', 'vegetables' instead of 'Gemüse'.
All annotations use English forms: 'regularly', 'as', 'a', 'preventive', 'measure', 'against', 'mosquitoes', etc. Should reference German text forms.
Annotations use English forms: 'discovers', 'diabetes', 'injects', 'insulin', 'daily'. Should reference German text forms: 'entdeckt', 'Diabetes', 'spritzt', 'Insulin', 'täglich'.

Annotation for last 'ein' has pos 'verb' gloss_hint 'inject' — this is the verbal prefix of 'einspritzen' (separable verb). The annotation conflates the separated prefix with the main verb. The verb 'spritzt...ein' should be annotated as lemma 'einspritzen' not 'spritzen'. However this example is owned by spritzen, so this is a minor inconsistency.

Multiple annotations use English lemmas: 'five' instead of 'fünf', 'water' instead of 'Wasser', 'approximately' instead of 'etwa', 'on' instead of 'auf', 'or' instead of 'bzw.', 'which' instead of 'das', 'reactor building' instead of 'Reaktorgebäude'.

Translation 'Spray it in my face' is wrong for the 'ejaculate' sense (sense 7). In this sexual context, 'Spritz mir ins Gesicht' means 'Ejaculate on my face'. The translation should reflect the vulgar meaning.
Translation is null — untranslated example. Text: 'Sie spritzen Wein mit Leitungswasser?' = 'You dilute wine with tap water?'

gloss_en 'fellow (student)' is inaccurate for Studienreferendar. A Studienreferendar is a trainee teacher / student teacher, not a 'fellow'. The gloss_en_full 'Student teacher; trainee teacher' is correct. gloss_en should be 'trainee teacher'.
gloss_en 'pesticide' is wrong POS — it's a noun, but spritzen is a verb. Should be 'spray (pesticide)' or 'spray crops'.
Removed 'ejaculation' (noun, not verb synonym). Added 'come' and 'climax' as verb synonyms for the ejaculate sense.
